summary: The erdos_problems dataset contains 1,217 problems (1,179 open) with proof-tracking issues distributed evenly across ten quarters (q1-q10), each holding 100 issues. Visible per-problem detail is currently limited to erdos_0001 and erdos_0002, both of which have resolved proof issues, suggesting the dataset's broader activity is captured in the quarterly aggregates rather than surfaced at the individual-problem level. No problem shows disproportionate traction in the available sample, and metadata richness (prizes, tags, OEIS, status) is underutilized in the current view.
problems: Per-problem issue visibility is restricted to erdos_0001 and erdos_0002; the other 1,215 problems lack surfaced issue breakdowns
Proof status is reported only as undifferentiated quarterly buckets (q1-q10, 100 issues each), making it impossible to attribute progress to specific problems
Both visible problems carry '(untitled)' proof issues, indicating missing or incomplete issue titles across the dataset
The even 100-per-quarter distribution looks like a sampling or pagination artifact rather than genuine activity signal
No linkage shown between the 1,000 quarterly issues and concrete Erdős problem IDs, breaking traceability
Solved/disproved/open status flags and cash-prize fields are not joined to the issue data, hiding which active problems are high-value
highlights: erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 each have a resolved proof issue, demonstrating that the resolution workflow functions end-to-end
The dataset is well-scoped and authoritative (maintained by Terence Tao) with 1,179 open problems available for prioritization
Rich metadata exists (prizes, tags, OEIS references, status) that can power high-signal prioritization once joined to issues
A consistent 1,000 tracked proof issues across q1-q10 indicates broad, structured engagement coverage

suggested_todos.title: Join quarterly proof issues (q1-q10) back to specific Erdős problem IDs to restore traceability
Backfill descriptive titles for '(untitled)' proof issues starting with erdos_0001/0002 pattern
Build a leaderboard of problems ranked by open-issue count and cash-prize value to identify highest-traction targets
Flag near-resolution candidates by surfacing problems whose only remaining issues are review/verification stage
Cross-reference OEIS-tagged problems to spotlight those with strong external computational support for likely near-term solving
Audit the even 100-per-quarter distribution to confirm it reflects real activity rather than a pagination cap
Generate a weekly digest of newly resolved problems modeled on the erdos_0001/0002 resolution pattern
